chunk_summary: Guidelines for protein intake in adolescents focus on per-meal targets, vegetarian protein quality, and practical strategies for growth, recovery, and weight management.
learning_objectives: Understand per-meal protein distribution for adolescent growth and recovery.
Learn strategies to improve protein quality in vegetarian diets.
Identify protein intake adjustments for athletic training or obesity management.
key_facts: Adolescents require at least 0.85 g/kg/day of protein, with higher targets (1.2–2.0 g/kg/day) for athletic training or catch-up growth.
Distribute protein intake as 0.25 g/kg per meal (20–40 g) every 3–4 hours across meals and snacks.
Vegetarian diets should combine cereals with pulses and include dairy/soy to improve essential amino acid density.
Vitamin B12 sources (dairy, eggs, fortified foods) or supplementation are critical for vegetarian adolescents.
Portable protein options like milk tetra packs, curd cups, or roasted chana are recommended when schedules or appetite limit intake.
For adolescents with obesity, maintain protein and dairy intake while reducing refined cereals, sweetened beverages, and ultra-processed snacks.
reference_quote: Position of the Academy of Nutrition and Dietetics: Vegetarian Diets — 'Vegetarian diets, when appropriately planned, are healthful, nutritionally adequate, and may provide health benefits for the prevention and treatment of certain diseases.'
document_key_topics: Improving protein quality in vegetarian diets
Adjusting protein intake for athletic training or obesity
Practical strategies for protein adequacy in India
